author: sycamore-yu
text:
Implemented and validated the missing runtime differentiable propagation path.

Summary:
- The runtime now uses Newton-backed state propagation with an explicit autograd bridge instead of only calling Newton with requires_grad disabled.
- DroneEnv now enables differentiable dynamics by default via DroneConfig(requires_grad=True) for the training path.
- Added a lightweight IsaacLab compatibility layer so tests do not hard-fail on IsaacSim TLS/UI bootstrap in headless environments.
- Aligned env.step and SHAC rollout collection around the tested contract: obs, (loss, reward), terminated, truncated, extras.
- Default runtime device now prefers CUDA when available.

Key files:
- diffaero_newton/source/diffaero_newton/dynamics/drone_dynamics.py
- diffaero_newton/source/diffaero_newton/envs/drone_env.py
- diffaero_newton/source/diffaero_newton/training/shac.py
- diffaero_newton/source/diffaero_newton/configs/drone_env_cfg.py
- diffaero_newton/source/diffaero_newton/common/isaaclab_compat.py

Validation:
- conda run -n isaaclab-newton pytest -q diffaero_newton/tests/test_obstacle_training.py
- Result: 18 passed, 4 warnings

Interpretation:
- Current quadrotor rollout is now Newton-backed and differentiable on the tested obstacle-training path.
- This does not mean the full DiffAero migration is complete; it only closes the runtime differentiable propagation gap for the current obstacle + SHAC vertical slice.
